name: Architecture Acceptance Profile Proof Gate
purpose: Produce direct, Module-owned Evidence for the Architecture Acceptance Profile Closure Contract through fixed test entrypoints that fail closed until their implementations exist.

oracle.description: The fixed Project Model Architecture Acceptance Profile proof test entrypoint must exist and exit zero.

rejects: a missing fixed Project Model profile proof test entrypoint
unbounded or nondeterministic Architecture Acceptance Profile compilation
lossy, dangling, or invented relations across Outcome, Criterion, Claim, Gate, Evidence, Residual Uncertainty, and Knowledge Gap
Outcome Contributions represented as Criterion satisfaction

residualUncertainty: Profile compilation evaluates supplied snapshots; source stabilization and Evidence currency classification remain Change Kernel responsibilities.

oracle.description: The fixed Change Kernel Architecture Acceptance Profile proof test entrypoint must exist and exit zero.

rejects: a missing fixed Change Kernel profile proof test entrypoint
Architecture Acceptance Profiles emitted from an unstable Project Model, Git-content, or Change Store source set
Evidence classified as current without exact Project Model, Git-content, and verification-subject bindings
current Evidence conflated with sealed historical or invalid Evidence

residualUncertainty: Snapshot stabilization covers one local single-writer workspace; Accepted history is neither portable across fresh clones nor authenticated for multi-writer use.

oracle.description: The fixed Local Workbench Architecture Acceptance Profile proof test entrypoint must exist and exit zero.

rejects: a missing fixed Local Workbench profile proof test entrypoint
CLI, HTTP, or browser view-model loss or conflation of Outcome, Criterion, Claim, Gate, Evidence currency, Residual Uncertainty, and Knowledge Gap
aggregate scores, percentages, confidence, or green-light status that collapse distinct assurance dimensions
top-level profile payloads that include source, package, stdout, or stderr bodies instead of bounded references

residualUncertainty: Browser DOM rendering and interaction remain outside this proof Gate; the browser view model is verified without a real browser.
The real cross-interface HTTP-to-Kernel lifecycle remains a full-Gate concern.
Fresh clones and other workspaces cannot reconstruct ignored local Accepted history; cross-workspace profile portability remains an explicit Knowledge Gap.
